text: The surgeon passes a surgical stapler through the bottom end of the rectal stump, which appears rounded and is visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon connects the rectal stump to an anvil, which has been inserted into the surgical field, preparing for the next step in the rectopexy procedure.

The surgeon uses a circular stapler to join the proximal colon and rectal stump. As the stapler is tightened, the anvil and stapler head visibly come together, indicating the alignment of the colon and rectum for anastomosis.
The surgeon fires the circular knife, cutting through the apposed sections of the colon and rectum, completing the anastomosis.
The surgeon observes the head of the circular stapler flattening as it is pulled out from the rectal stump.
The surgeon completes the anastomosis using a laparoscopic technique, joining the corresponding sections of bowel with precision.
